name: AI Vendor Risk Assessment Framework
description: Proprietary framework for assessing AI/ML vendor risks including model poisoning, bias detection, data provenance, and AI-specific security controls. Built on NIST AI RMF principles with additional vendor-specific assessments.

categories.name: Data Provenance
categories.description: Controls for training data documentation, licensing, data quality, and PII handling in AI systems.

controls.name: Training Data Documentation
controls.description: Does the vendor document the sources, composition, and characteristics of training data?

requirements.description: Maintain comprehensive documentation of training data sources and collection methods.
requirements.guidance: Review data cards, source documentation, and data collection procedures.

requirements.description: Document data composition including demographic distributions and potential biases.
requirements.guidance: Examine data statistics, distribution analyses, and bias assessments.

requirements.description: Track data versioning and changes over time.
requirements.guidance: Review version control, change logs, and data lineage documentation.

controls.name: Data Licensing and Rights
controls.description: Does the vendor have appropriate rights and licenses for training data?

requirements.description: Verify legal rights to use training data for AI model development.
requirements.guidance: Review licensing agreements, consent documentation, and legal assessments.

requirements.description: Document third-party data sources and associated licensing terms.
requirements.guidance: Examine vendor agreements, data purchase contracts, and usage rights.

controls.name: PII and Sensitive Data Handling
controls.description: How does the vendor handle personally identifiable information in training data?

requirements.description: Implement data anonymization or de-identification procedures for training data.
requirements.guidance: Review anonymization techniques, k-anonymity measures, and PII detection.

requirements.description: Document consent mechanisms for data subjects whose data is used in training.
requirements.guidance: Examine consent records, opt-out mechanisms, and privacy notices.

requirements.description: Implement data retention policies specific to AI training data.
requirements.guidance: Review retention schedules, deletion procedures, and compliance documentation.

controls.name: Data Quality Assurance
controls.description: What data quality controls are in place for AI training data?

requirements.description: Implement data validation and cleansing procedures before training.
requirements.guidance: Review data quality metrics, validation rules, and cleansing procedures.

requirements.description: Monitor for data drift and quality degradation over time.
requirements.guidance: Examine drift detection mechanisms, quality dashboards, and alerting.

categories.name: Model Poisoning Prevention
categories.description: Controls to prevent and detect adversarial attacks on AI model training pipelines.

controls.name: Training Pipeline Security
controls.description: Are AI training pipelines protected against unauthorized modifications?

requirements.description: Implement access controls and authentication for training pipeline components.
requirements.guidance: Review access control policies, authentication mechanisms, and audit logs.

requirements.description: Sign and verify training data, code, and model artifacts.
requirements.guidance: Examine digital signatures, integrity checks, and verification procedures.

requirements.description: Monitor training pipelines for anomalous behavior.
requirements.guidance: Review monitoring dashboards, anomaly detection, and alerting mechanisms.

controls.name: Data Integrity Protection
controls.description: How is training data protected from tampering or poisoning?

requirements.description: Implement cryptographic integrity checks for training data.
requirements.guidance: Review hashing, checksums, and tamper detection mechanisms.

requirements.description: Detect and filter potentially poisoned or adversarial samples.
requirements.guidance: Examine outlier detection, sample validation, and filtering procedures.

controls.name: Model Artifact Security
controls.description: Are trained model artifacts protected and verified?

requirements.description: Store model artifacts in secure, access-controlled repositories.
requirements.guidance: Review model storage security, access controls, and encryption.

requirements.description: Verify model integrity before deployment.
requirements.guidance: Examine deployment verification, signature checks, and integrity validation.

categories.name: Bias Detection and Fairness
categories.description: Controls for detecting, measuring, and mitigating bias in AI systems.

controls.name: Bias Assessment
controls.description: Does the vendor conduct bias assessments on AI models?

requirements.description: Conduct pre-deployment bias testing across protected characteristics.
requirements.guidance: Review bias test results, demographic analysis, and fairness metrics.

requirements.description: Document identified biases and their potential impacts.
requirements.guidance: Examine bias documentation, impact assessments, and disclosure practices.

requirements.description: Implement ongoing bias monitoring in production.
requirements.guidance: Review monitoring dashboards, bias metrics, and alerting thresholds.

controls.name: Fairness Metrics
controls.description: What fairness metrics does the vendor track and report?

requirements.description: Define and track appropriate fairness metrics for the use case.
requirements.guidance: Review fairness metric definitions, measurements, and targets.

requirements.description: Report fairness metrics to stakeholders transparently.
requirements.guidance: Examine reporting procedures, stakeholder communication, and disclosure.

controls.name: Bias Mitigation
controls.description: What processes exist to mitigate identified biases?

requirements.description: Implement bias mitigation techniques in model development.
requirements.guidance: Review mitigation techniques, retraining procedures, and effectiveness testing.

requirements.description: Document bias mitigation decisions and trade-offs.
requirements.guidance: Examine decision documentation, trade-off analyses, and approval records.

categories.name: Model Security
categories.description: Security controls specific to AI model deployment and operation.

controls.name: Model Access Control
controls.description: How is access to AI models controlled and monitored?

requirements.description: Implement authentication and authorization for model access.
requirements.guidance: Review access policies, authentication mechanisms, and role definitions.

requirements.description: Log and audit all model invocations and access attempts.
requirements.guidance: Examine audit logs, access reports, and monitoring dashboards.

requirements.description: Implement rate limiting and abuse detection.
requirements.guidance: Review rate limits, anomaly detection, and blocking mechanisms.

controls.name: Adversarial Robustness
controls.description: Is the model tested against adversarial attacks?

requirements.description: Conduct adversarial testing to evaluate model robustness.
requirements.guidance: Review adversarial test results, attack scenarios, and robustness metrics.

requirements.description: Implement input validation and sanitization.
requirements.guidance: Examine input validation rules, sanitization procedures, and edge case handling.

controls.name: Model Extraction Prevention
controls.description: What controls prevent model theft or extraction?

requirements.description: Implement controls to prevent model extraction through API queries.
requirements.guidance: Review query limits, watermarking, and extraction detection mechanisms.

requirements.description: Monitor for suspicious query patterns indicating extraction attempts.
requirements.guidance: Examine monitoring rules, detection algorithms, and response procedures.

categories.name: Explainability and Transparency
categories.description: Controls for AI model explainability, interpretability, and transparency.

controls.name: Model Documentation
controls.description: Does the vendor provide comprehensive model documentation?

requirements.description: Provide model cards or equivalent documentation for all deployed models.
requirements.guidance: Review model cards, technical documentation, and use case specifications.

requirements.description: Document model limitations, known failure modes, and appropriate use cases.
requirements.guidance: Examine limitation documentation, failure mode analysis, and usage guidance.

controls.name: Decision Explainability
controls.description: Can model decisions be explained to stakeholders?

requirements.description: Provide explanation capabilities for model outputs when required.
requirements.guidance: Review explanation mechanisms, interpretability tools, and output documentation.

requirements.description: Enable human review of high-stakes decisions.
requirements.guidance: Examine human-in-the-loop processes, review procedures, and override capabilities.

controls.name: Algorithmic Transparency
controls.description: What information is disclosed about the AI system's operation?

requirements.description: Disclose when AI is being used in decision-making processes.
requirements.guidance: Review disclosure practices, user notifications, and transparency statements.

requirements.description: Provide information about factors influencing AI decisions.
requirements.guidance: Examine factor documentation, feature importance, and decision rationale.

categories.name: Privacy Protection
categories.description: Privacy-specific controls for AI systems beyond general data protection.

controls.name: Inference Privacy
controls.description: Are measures in place to protect privacy during model inference?

requirements.description: Minimize data collection during model inference.
requirements.guidance: Review data collection practices, retention policies, and minimization techniques.

requirements.description: Protect inference queries and responses from unauthorized access.
requirements.guidance: Examine encryption, access controls, and data handling procedures.

controls.name: Membership Inference Prevention
controls.description: Are controls in place to prevent membership inference attacks?

requirements.description: Assess risk of membership inference on deployed models.
requirements.guidance: Review risk assessments, attack testing, and vulnerability analyses.

requirements.description: Implement differential privacy or similar protections where appropriate.
requirements.guidance: Examine privacy-preserving techniques, implementation evidence, and effectiveness.

controls.name: Model Inversion Prevention
controls.description: What protections exist against model inversion attacks?

requirements.description: Assess and mitigate model inversion risks.
requirements.guidance: Review inversion attack testing, risk mitigation, and protection mechanisms.
